California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 
■5′  ■99■
Lorraine D′ Elia
City Clerk
City of Da■y city
333 90th Street
Daly city′  CA  940■5-■895
Re:
Your Request for lnformal Ass■
stance
Our File No. 
■-90-773
Dear Ms. D'EIia:
This Letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the permissible uses of carnpaign funds under the
provisions of the Pol,itical Reform Act (the nActtr).f Your request
is general in nature and seeks general g:uidance concerning the
permissible uses of campaign funds upon the reel,ection or defeat
of incumbent candidates. Accordingly, we treat your letter as a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c)
(copy enclosed).2
OUESTIONS
l-. Under the provisions of the Act, when an incumbent is not
reelected and has campaign funds renaining in his or her carnpaign
bank account, may the incumbent donate funds to charity,
contrj-bute to other candidates, and transfer funds to a new
account for a future candidacy to another office?
2. Under the provisions of the Act, rrhen an incumbent is
reelected, during which period of time nay the incurnbent transfer
1 Gor".r,."nt code Sections 8l,ooo-910L5. Al-l statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. Al,I references to regulations are to Title 
2
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informa] assistance does not provide the requestor srith the
inmunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114 i Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8OZ o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8OZ . (916)322.5660


Our Fi■e No. ■-90-773
Page 2
funds in the campaign bank account to a new account for a future
candidacy to another office?
CONCLUS ION
1. when an incumbent who has carnpaign funds rernaining in a
campaign bank account is not reelected to office, the .incurnbent
may- donate the funds to charity or contribute them to federal or
oul-of-state candidates. However, the incurnbent may only transfer
the funds to a new account for his or her future candidacy prior
to the t j,me the funds become rrsurpfusrr funds.3 Th" funds becone
trsurplus, at the end of the posteLection reporting period.
2. when an incumbent is reelected, the incumbent may
transfer funds in the ca:npaign bank account to a new account for a
future candidacy to another office at any time before the funds
become surplus funds.
ANALYSI S
The permissible use of campaign funds is governed by Sections
89510 thr6ugh 89521. In order to ascertain the applicability of
these statutory provisions to a particular candidate, it must
first 
be determined whether the candidate is an officeholder or
whether the candidate has been defeated in his or her quest for
elective office.
Following the defeat of a candidate for elective office,
campaign fundi under the control of the candidate become surplus
tunas it the end of the posteJ.ection reporting period. 
(section
89519.) The reporting periods end on June 30 and December 31 of
each year. (Section 8420o. )
The commission has adopted Regulation 18587 (copy enclosed)
which provides guidance in determining when campaign funds become
"surplus funds.r' This regulation states in pertinent part that:
(a) Campaign funds raised after January 1,
1989, under the control of a former candidate or
elected officer shall be considered surpLus
campaign funds on the following dates:
(1) fncumbent candidates: The date on
which an incumbent candidate l-eaves any
elective office for which the canpaign funds
were raised.
' 
Th" advice provided erith respect to surplus funds applies only
to funds which are considered to have been raised after January 1,
1989. Surplus funds which were raised prior to 1989 and were not
commingled with funds raised after January l-, 1989, are governed
by provisions of the Electi-on Code. Questions regarding such
funds should be directed to the Office of the Attorney General.


Our File No. 
■-90-773
Page 3
(2) Defeated candidates: The end of the
postelection reporting period following the
defeat of a candidate for elective office for
which the campaign funds were raised.
(b) The riend of the postelection reporting
periodrr means June 30 with respect to elections
occurring in the first six months of the calendar
year and December 3l- for elections occurring in the
latter six months of the calendar year.
(c) Campaign funds are rrraisedrr at the tine
the funds are received, as disclosed on the
candidate's carnpaign statements Pursuant to
covernment code Section 84211-
(d) Carnpaign funds raised before January 1,
1989, which have been commingled with canpaign
funds raised on or after January 1, 1989, are
presumed to have been raised on or after January 1,
1989.
ReguJ.ation 18587.
Once campaign funds become surplus, they nay be used only for
the following PurPoses:
(a) The pa)rnent of outstanding campaign debts
or elected officer's 
expenses.
(b) The pro rata repayment of contributi-ons.
(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, !,rhere no
substantial part of the proceeds will have a
material financial effect on the former candidate
or elected officer, any nember of his or her
irnrnediate farnily, or his or her campaign treasurer.
(d) Contributions to a pofitical party or
cornmittee so long as the funds are not used to make
contributions in support of or opposition to a
candidate for elective office.
(e) Contributions to support or oppose any
candidate for federal office, any candidate for
elective office in a state other than California,
or any ballot measure.
Section 895■9.


Our File No. 
■-90-773
Page 4
No other use is permitted. (See Leece Advice Letter, No. I-9o-
061; Shade Advice Letter, No. A-90-449; Hefter Advice Letter, No.
T-9o-582 (copies enclosed).
Accordingly, once the funds become trsurplus,rt they rnay not be
used to fund a candidate's future campaign for etective office nor
may the funds be used to rnake contributions to other candidates
running for a catifornia elective office. 
Donations to charity,
howevei, are permissibte if they faI1 under one of the categories
tisted in subsection (c) above. Contributions to federal and out-
of-state candidates are also permissible. Prior to the tirne when
the funds becone rrsurPfusir funds, applicable l-aw does not
distinguish betlreen defeated candidates and those who have been
reelected to office. 
Therefore, the foLlowing discussion applies
to both.
Until such time as the funds becorne rrsurplusrI candidates may
transfer funds between their o$rn control-Led cornnittees. As
enacted by Proposition 73, section 85304 prohibits candj-dates or
officeholders frorn transferring funds among their own controlled
committees. However, the United States District court, Eastern
District of California invalidated the transfer prohibitions of
section 85304 to the extent the prohibitions serve to further the
contribution limitations based on a fiscal year scheme also found
i-nvalid by the court. 
(Service Emplovees International Union'
AFL―C10. et al. v. Fair political Practices Commission (Case
No. CIVS-89-0433 LKK―
」FM)  (■SEIU::)。
)
Therefore, a candidate
transfer funds from one cornmittee to a ne$, account for his or
future candidacy to another offj.ce. This transfer nay be rnade
any tirne prior to the tine when the funds become rrsurplusrr as
discussed above.
may
her
at
The Act, as anended by Proposition 73, requires that
candidates establish one campaign bank account for each office
they seek. (Section 85201.) Section 84101 requires candidates to
file a statement of organization within 10 days after receipt of
contributions totaling S1,000 or more in a calendar year.
commission regulations further require the establishment of a
separate controlled cornrnittee for each campaign bank account:
When a candidate is required to file 
a
statement of organization for a controlled
comrnittee pursuant to Government code Section
841oL, the candidate shall establish a separate
controlled cornrnittee for each specific office
identified in statements filed by the candidate
pursuant to covernnent Code Section 85200.
Regulation 18521 (copy
enclosed).
4 Th" decision in
to change pending
SEfU has been appealed. Our advice is subject
the outcome of this appeal.


Our Fi■e No. エー90-773
Page 5
A candidate nay designate any committee and any bank account
established prior to January 1-, 1989, for any office and el-ection
for which he or she intends to receive contributions, so long as
the connittee does not have any outstanding debts owed from a
previous election. 
This is true even if the committee and account
currently have campaign funds.
A committee and bank account established after January 1,
1989, may be designated for reelection to the sarne office. 
This
is true even if the committee and account have carnpaign funds and/
or outstanding debts from the previous election. 
The officeholder
rnust fite a new candidate intention Form 501, must amend the bank
account statement Form 502 to designate the account for the next
election, and must amend the committee's statement of organization
(Forn 410) if necessary to indicate that it has been designated
for the next election.
A candidate who has a bank account and comnittee for one
office and decides to run for another office must file Form 501,
open a new bank account, and file a new Forn 502. The candidate
will- also have to file a ne$/ Form 410 unless he or she wants to
transfer aII funds frorn the o1d account, close it, and designate
the connittee for the new account. No debts rnay be owed by or to
the cornrnittee fron a previous election.
Thus, both an incurnbent and a defeated candidate may transfer
carnpaign funds to a new account to run for future office. 
When a
candidate has been defeated, however, this transfer is onJ-y
permissible prior to the time the funds becone trsurpl,usrr funds,
we trust this letter adeguately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this natter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-59OL.
sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel
By: Blanca !1. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division
SH:BMB:aa
Enc losures
